question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's a complete breakdown of the `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm, sourced from the [official Charmcraft documentation](https://documentation.ubuntu.com/charmcraft/stable/reference/files/charmcraft-yaml-file/).

---

## Minimal Kubernetes Charm Example

This covers all **required** fields to get a Kubernetes charm working:

```yaml
name: my-app-k8s           # Use -k8s suffix by convention
type: charm                # Always "charm"
base: ubuntu@24.04
summary: A one-line summary (max 78 chars).
description: |
  A longer description of what this charm does.
  Displayed on Charmhub.

assumes:
  - k8s-api                # Requires a Kubernetes model
  - juju >= 3.3

platforms:
  amd64:

containers:
  app:
    resource: app-image    # Must reference a resource below

resources:
  app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the application

parts:
  charm:
    plugin: charm
    source: .
```

---

## Complete Example (All Fields)

```yaml
# ── Identity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
name: my-app-k8s
type: charm
title: My Awesome App           # Human-readable name for Charmhub
summary: Deploys My App on Kubernetes.
description: |
  A longer, multiline description of the charm shown on Charmhub.

# ── Runtime base ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
base: ubuntu@24.04
# build-base: ubuntu@24.04     # Only needed if base is ubuntu@devel

# ── Target platforms / architectures ──────────────────────────────────────────
platforms:
  amd64:
    build-on: [amd64]
    build-for: [amd64]
  arm64:
    build-on: [amd64, arm64]
    build-for: [arm64]

# ── Feature requirements ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
assumes:
  - k8s-api                     # ← Required for Kubernetes charms
  - juju >= 3.3

# ── Kubernetes: containers & OCI images (REQUIRED for K8s) ───────────────────
containers:
  app:
    resource: app-image         # References an oci-image resource below
    mounts:
      - storage: data           # References a storage entry below
        location: /var/lib/app
    uid: 1000                   # Non-root user (Juju 3.5+)
    gid: 1000

resources:
  app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the application (docker.io/myorg/my-app)

# ── Privilege context (Kubernetes only, Juju 3.6+) ────────────────────────────
charm-user: non-root            # root | sudoer | non-root

# ── User-facing config options ────────────────────────────────────────────────
config:
  options:
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on.
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: Logging level (debug, info, warning, error).
    tls-cert:
      type: secret
      description: TLS certificate secret URI.

# ── Charm actions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  restart:
    description: Restart the application container.
  backup:
    description: Create a backup of application data.
    params:
      destination:
        type: string
        description: Path to write the backup.
    required: [destination]
    additionalProperties: false

# ── Integration endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
provides:
  http:
    interface: http

requires:
  db:
    interface: postgresql_client
    limit: 1
    optional: false
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-peers

# ── Storage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent storage for application data.

# ── Charmhub metadata ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
links:
  documentation: https://documentation.ubuntu.com/...
  issues:
    - https://github.com/myorg/my-app-k8s/issues
  source:
    - https://github.com/myorg/my-app-k8s
  website:
    - https://myapp.example.com

# ── Library dependencies from Charmhub ────────────────────────────────────────
charm-libs:
  - lib: postgresql.postgres_client
    version: "1"
  - lib: nginx_ingress_integrator.nginx_ingress_integrator
    version: "0"

# ── Build configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
parts:
  charm:
    plugin: charm
    source: .
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements: [requirements.txt]
    charm-strict-dependencies: false
```

---

## Key Rules for Kubernetes Charms

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`containers` is required** | Every K8s charm (except proxy charms) must declare at least one container |
| **`resources` with `oci-image`** | Every container in `containers` must reference a named `oci-image` resource |
| **`assumes: [k8s-api]`** | Strongly recommended — prevents accidental deployment on machine models |
| **`platforms`** | Replaces the deprecated `bases` field; specifies target CPU architectures |
| **`charm-user`** | K8s-only (Juju 3.6+): controls hook privilege — `root`, `sudoer`, or `non-root` |
| **`uid`/`gid` on containers** | K8s-only (Juju 3.5+): sets the workload container's process user |
| **Name convention** | Use a `-k8s` suffix (e.g., `my-app-k8s`) to distinguish from machine charms |
| **`type: charm`** | Always required — tells Charmcraft this is a charm (not a bundle or snap) |

The only fields that are **strictly required** to pack a valid Kubernetes charm are: `name`, `type`, `base`, `summary`, `description`, `platforms`, `containers`, `resources`, and `parts`.
